$doc: COMMITTED reference roster for the review council. It is NOT the runtime config: at setup, the installer resolves each provider role to its CURRENT flagship model slug and the transport chosen for this machine, then writes local/seats.<host>.json (gitignored). Model slugs are NEVER committed here — recipes use the {MODEL} placeholder so this file never goes stale. {EFFORT} = efforts.default below the critical tier, efforts.max at critical. {PROMPT_TEXT} = the review prompt content (arg transport); stdin transport omits it and pipes the prompt file.

roster.modelGuidance: The Anthropic OPUS line specifically. Use the `opus` alias (tracks the latest Opus) or a concrete claude-opus-* id. NEVER Fable or Mythos (the Claude-5 / Mythos-class line) — setup must confirm the resolved slug is an Opus id.
roster.recursionDisable: --safe-mode (mechanically disables CLAUDE.md, skills, plugins, hooks, MCP)

roster.modelGuidance: The latest Codex/GPT flagship (e.g. gpt-5.5, then gpt-5.6, ...). Resolve at setup.
roster.recursionDisable: isolated neutral CODEX_HOME (empty dir: no hooks.json, skills, or global AGENTS.md) + --sandbox read-only

asNative.note: native pass uses the host's own configured model (no -m); it IS the host's model by definition

roster.modelGuidance: Latest GLM (e.g. z-ai/glm-5.2) via OpenRouter. Resolve at setup.
roster.recursionDisable: opencode --agent plan (read-only) + clean config dir; opencode does not load CLAUDE.md-style council mandates

roster.modelGuidance: Latest Gemini flagship (e.g. google/gemini-3.1-pro) via OpenRouter. Native Gemini-CLI plan mode is experimental, so OpenCode+OpenRouter is the firm read-only route. Resolve at setup.
Latest Grok flagship (e.g. grok-4.5 in Cursor, or x-ai/grok-4.5 via OpenRouter). For cursor-agent, confirm the exact slug with `cursor-agent --list-models`. Resolve at setup.
roster.recursionDisable: cursor-agent --mode plan (read-only) in a clean project dir; or opencode --agent plan via OpenRouter

$runtimeSchema.$doc: Shape of the machine-local local/seats.<host>.json the installer writes and the council skill reads. `native` describes how THIS host runs its own-model read-only review pass; `seats` are the EXTERNAL seats = roster minus this host's own provider, strongest-first, with {MODEL} already resolved to a concrete slug.
